channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Cardi B and Stefon Diggs Reunite at Mother’s Day Event
item.description: American rapper Cardi B and NFL star Stefon Diggs were spotted together at the Diggs Deep Foundation’s Mother’s Day event, sparking rumours that they might have reconciled. The couple had reportedly u...

item.title: Key Matches That Will Determine the Premier League Champion Between Arsenal and Manchester City
item.description: With just two weeks left in the Premier League, the title race between Arsenal and Manchester City is tighter than ever, with only a two-point gap separating the two teams. Manchester City boosted the...

item.title: Petroleum dealers and Supreme Court Bar Association condemn high petroleum levies and fuel price increase
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Pakistan Petroleum Dealers Association (PPDA) and the Supreme Court Bar Association (SCBA) have strongly criticised the federal government for the recent increase in fuel prices and hea...

item.title: Cruise Ship Arrives in Tenerife Amid Hantavirus Outbreak, Passengers Evacuated Safely
item.description: The cruise ship MV Hondius, linked to a deadly hantavirus outbreak, arrived at Tenerife early Sunday, prompting a coordinated evacuation of passengers. Spanish health authorities will screen passenger...

item.title: Corporate regulator investigated Hudson Global before administration
item.description: Hudson Global, one of Australia's largest public sector recruitment firms, was under investigation by the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC) prior to entering voluntary administra...

item.title: Understanding Lupus: Why Women Are More Vulnerable to This Autoimmune Disease
item.description: Lupus is a chronic autoimmune disorder where the body’s immune system mistakenly attacks its own tissues and organs. It is often referred to as an ‘invisible illness’ because early symptoms like fatig...

item.title: Researchers Identify Neural Mechanism That Limits Scratching Behavior
item.description: Researchers at the University of Louvain in Brussels have discovered a key mechanism in the nervous system that tells the brain when to stop scratching an itch. The study, presented at the 70th Biophy...

item.title: Lagos Police Successfully Rescue Four Abducted Children
item.description: On May 8, 2026, Lagos State Police Command officers successfully rescued four children wey dem don kidnap from Ijesha-Tedo area of Lagos. The children dey with one unidentified woman near Alafia Bus T...

item.title: Minister Judith Kapinga urges new factories to hire local Tanzanians for stronger economy
item.description: The Minister for Industry and Trade, Ms Judith Kapinga, has called upon owners of new factories in Tanzania to make sure they employ more Tanzanians. This move is aimed at boosting the national econom...

item.title: Restoring La Concorde: Preserving Paarl’s Heritage for the Future
item.description: La Concorde in Paarl is more than just an office building; it is a historical landmark that embodies the legacy of the KWV and the South African wine industry. Originally built between 1956 and 1958, ...

item.title: UK local elections highlight growing political fragmentation and challenges for Labour
item.description: Recent local elections across England, Scotland, and Wales have intensified pressures on Sir Keir Starmer's Labour leadership, signalling deep structural shifts in UK politics. The results indicate a ...

item.title: Global oil reserves shrinking rapidly amid Iran conflict, raising risk of shortages
item.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran is causing a significant drawdown of global oil inventories, particularly affecting the Persian Gulf supply. Between March 1 and April 25, global stockpiles fell by an est...

item.title: Photographic insight into Uganda's mountain gorilla census
item.description: The latest census of mountain gorillas in Uganda’s Bwindi Impenetrable National Park has provided a crucial glimpse into the population of these endangered primates. National Geographic photographer a...

item.title: SSGC Suspends Gas Supply in Multiple Karachi Localities Following High-Pressure Pipeline Damage During Red Line BRT Work
item.description: KARACHI: Gas supply to several major areas of Karachi was suspended on Saturday evening after a high-pressure gas pipeline was accidentally damaged during excavation work for the Red Line Bus Rapid Tr...

item.title: Final Evacuation Flights Planned for Hantavirus-Affected Cruise Ship in Canary Islands
item.description: The Spanish Health Minister, Monica Garcia, announced say the last evacuation flight for people wey dey on top cruise ship affected by deadly hantavirus go comot from Canary Islands on Monday. The Dut...

item.title: Aliko Dangote Eyes Mombasa for East Africa's Largest Oil Refinery
item.description: Aliko Dangote, the richest industrialist in Africa, has expressed his interest in building a massive oil refinery in Kenya, preferring Mombasa over Tanga, Tanzania. The proposed refinery is expected t...

item.title: Investigation reveals R2bn fraud through Ekurhuleni municipal IT system breach
item.description: In a major cybercrime operation, Ekurhuleni’s municipal IT billing system, SOLAR, was infiltrated, enabling a fraud totalling around R2 billion. Investigations revealed that a syndicate, with insider ...

item.title: ANC scrambles to avert Ramaphosa impeachment over Phala Phala scandal amid GNU tensions
item.description: The African National Congress (ANC) finds itself in a state of urgency as it works to delay or completely avoid an impeachment process against President Cyril Ramaphosa linked to the long-running Phal...

item.title: Regina restaurants reopen patios as spring weather arrives
item.description: With spring finally arriving in Regina, local businesses are welcoming customers back to outdoor patios. Earl’s Kitchen and Bar and Copperhead Brewing Co. are leading the way, offering patrons a chanc...

item.title: Using literature and foresight to protect Cameroon’s endangered sacred animals
item.description: In western Cameroon, especially in the grasslands and highlands, certain animals like lions, leopards, elephants, buffaloes, porcupines, cowries, and the Bannerman’s turaco hold strong cultural and sp...
